view_meta: Secret references (env:VAR / file:/path) — resolution, consumers, rotation age. Values never enter TG.
purpose: SECRETS management view (configuration domain): a reference-only registry (env:VAR / file:/path — values never enter TG) with a hazard invariant banner, resolution/rotation stat tiles, a map-vs-territory rotation-horizon strip (observed age in steel vs dashed-indigo policy), a missing-reference alarm, a dense evidentiary table with per-row rotation-age bars and inline detail (fingerprint, ledger trail, consumers, gated rotate/verify), and a gated add-reference composer.
notes:
Verified: node --check passes and a DOM-shim smoke test (replicating the documented h()/svgEl() contract: class/html/on*/setAttribute, array flattening, null skipping) rendered the view and asserted 13 rows + 13 hidden detail rows, 2 MISSING chips, 10 horizon dots (13 − 2 missing − 1 auto-rotated), row click toggles detail open/closed with aria-expanded, facet filters (missing=2, due=4), and sort order (missing first, then over-policy, then warm).

Design decisions, mapped to the color language: rotation-age fill and horizon dots are OBSERVED age = territory steel, escalating to --partial (>=70% of policy) and --deviation (past policy); the policy threshold is a CLAIM about what should be, so it renders as a dashed indigo (--map) tick/line in both the per-row bars and the horizon strip, with a legend naming both. Status is color+glyph+label (● RESOLVED jade / ✕ MISSING red / ↻ AUTO-ROTATED green / ▲ over-policy); missing rows also get a red inset stripe on the first cell. The invariant banner and all gating microcopy use --hazard with a lock glyph. --accent appears only on interaction (row focus, chip/button hover, clickable ledger refs). All evidentiary content (references, fingerprints, timestamps, ledger refs, day counts) is --mono with tabular-nums.

Integration notes: detail is an inline attached evidence panel (hidden <tr> toggled per row) rather than the session drawer, since openSession(s) is session-shaped; this keeps detail on-page per the no-new-page rule. Consumer chips route('modules') and ledger refs route('ledger'), both guarded with typeof checks so the module degrades gracefully if those views are absent. Everything is prefixed sec-/SEC_ (no globals besides SEC_REFS, SEC_LOCK_SM/LG, and the sec* functions). The horizon strip is built directly with svgEl since no named chart-primitive signatures were provided in the brief; swap for a documented primitive if one fits. Fixture consumers use family/module ids consistent with the ~31-connector fleet (ing
